_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: To obtain wound healing and closure as early as possible in patients with Diabetic Foot Disease
To achieve complete remission and non-recurrence to lower the chances of amputations in patients with DFD

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
Complete Blood count, Blood glucose level, HbA1C Report

claim.label: Detailed Indoor Case papers along with treatment details
Post treatment photograph of affected foot

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the Complete Blood count, blood glucose, HbA1C report submitted?
Was the photograph of affected foot submitted?
Was the pre and post treatment photograph of affected foot suggestive of Diabetic foot?

cpd_claim.q: Was Detailed Indoor Case Papers with Treatment details submitted?
Was the post treatment blood glucose submitted?
Was the post treatment photograph of affected foot submitted?
Was the Detailed Discharge Summary submitted with the date of the follow-up mentioned?

common_queries: Pre-treatment photograph of affected foot missing or not suggestive of Diabetic Foot.
Post-treatment photograph of affected foot missing or not suggestive of Diabetic Foot.
Clinical notes missing, incomplete, or lacking evaluation findings, indications, or planned treatment.
Complete Blood count, Blood glucose level, or HbA1C Report missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case papers with treatment details missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Post-treatment glucose level not submitted at claim submission.
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or lacking date of follow-up.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: DNB/MS/MD/DM or equivalent (in General Medicine/Endocrinology/General surgery)
clinical_key_pointers: Identification of historical and/or physical findings of Diabetic Foot can improve the prognosis and lead to a favourable outcome through appropriate treatment and early referral.
Early detection and effective management of diabetic foot ulcers can reduce complications, including preventable amputations and possible mortality.

A thorough patient examination is required, covering general history, neuropathic and peripheral vascular symptoms, smoking history, general physical examination (height, weight), and detailed foot examination (skin, neurological, vascular, musculoskeletal, and footwear).
The tuning fork and monofilament tests are important in diagnosing loss of protective sensation (LOPS).
additional_information.comorbidity_management:
Glycaemic control: Aim to maintain blood glucose between 140 and 180 mg/dl, individualized glycaemic targets, and HbA1c monitoring at least twice a year. Diet, exercise, and education form the foundation of T2DM therapy.
Infection management: Use of appropriate antimicrobial therapy based on wound category, with specialist advice. May include agents against Gram-positive cocci (e.g., MRSA) and anti-anaerobic agents based on culture and sensitivity.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral to appropriate level of care as required for treatment under PMJAY.
